sub_topics.content: We Are Crackin' LLC organized December 29, 2016, wholly owned subsidiary of ERI. Substantially all assets pledged as collateral for certain debts of Parent Company. Sells franchises for Elmer's, Elmer's Kitchen, and Egg N' Joe. Also performs marketing and advertising services.

Adopted ASU 2016-13 (CECL - Financial Instruments Credit Losses, Topic 326) effective January 2, 2023. Replaced incurred loss methodology with expected credit loss methodology. Transition adjustment decreased allowance for credit losses by $4,270, resulting in allowance of $730. Cumulative effect increased beginning member's equity by $4,270.
GAAP requires management estimates affecting reported amounts. Key estimates include allowance for credit losses and carrying amounts of accounts receivable.
52 or 53-week accounting period. Final day is last Sunday of each calendar year (or first Sunday in January for 53-week periods). All three reported periods were 52-week periods.

Includes cash from franchise and advertising activities in interest-bearing accounts at a financial institution. Insured by FDIC up to $250,000. Advertising cash separately accounted and set aside for advertising expenditures per franchise agreements.

Primarily amounts due from franchisees. Recorded as services are rendered. Charged off against allowance when uncollectible. Allowance estimated based on historical losses, current conditions, reasonable and supportable forecasts. Company considers receivables past due after 30 days. No trade accounts receivable were past due as of December 29, 2024. Allowance: 1% of trade accounts receivable ($730) for periods ended Dec 29, 2024 and Dec 31, 2023. $5,000 for period ended January 1, 2023.

Opening balances of accounts receivable at Jan 1, 2024, Jan 2, 2023 and Jan 3, 2022 were $66,800, $67,529 and $84,448. Opening contract liabilities (unspent advertising funds) were $478,498, $442,802 and $317,123.

sub_topics.topic: Franchise Operations and Advertising Fee Revenue
sub_topics.content: Initial license fees from franchise and area development agreements recognized when restaurant opens. Continuing franchise fees (percentage of sales) recognized as revenue each month based on franchisees' monthly sales activity. Advertising fees received but not yet used deferred as liability (unspent advertising funds) until related expense incurred.

Agreements with several restaurant operators throughout Western US. Terms protect territory for operator, provide standard building blueprints, recipes, formulas, methods of food preparation, collaborative regional marketing and advertising. Fulfilled over franchise agreement terms (15 to 25 years). One franchise sale in period ended January 1, 2023, for $40,000 deferred income, recognized upon completion of all performance obligations at restaurant opening during period ended December 31, 2023. No franchise sales for periods ended December 29, 2024 and December 31, 2023.

Company receives variable consideration from supplier-based product purchases and usage. Revenues recognized at a point in time upon receipt of payment from supplier. Company does not estimate or accrue for these revenues due to variable nature of product usage.

Company pays management fee equal to 10% of revenue for support services provided by Parent Company. For periods ended Dec 29, 2024, Dec 31, 2023 and Jan 1, 2023, management fee was $124,634, $154,632 and $139,279, included in G&A expenses.

LLC under provisions of partnership taxation. Due to one sole member, considered a disregarded entity. Does not pay partnership income taxes on taxable income. Sole member responsible for individual income taxes, included in S-corporation filings. No provision for income taxes in financial statements. Reviewed potential tax uncertainties per FASB ASC 740 — no uncertainties with material impact for all three periods.
Financial instruments potentially subject to concentration consist principally of accounts receivable. Accounts receivable balances consist primarily of receivables from franchisees.

notes.title: Related Party Transactions
notes.content: Summary of related party transactions and balances

transactions.item: Management fee paid to Parent Company

notes.distribution_basis: quarterly distributions based on projected results
notes.equity_contribution_note: One equity contribution of $100,000 recorded as receivable for period ended January 1, 2023

notes.title: Commitments and Contingencies
notes.content: Cash balances held in financial institutions insured by FDIC up to $250,000. Company had uninsured deposits of $601,700, $779,892 and $308,662 for periods ended December 29, 2024, December 31, 2023 and January 1, 2023. This represents a credit risk to the Company.

notes.title: Reclassification
notes.content: Certain prior year amounts reclassified to conform to current year presentation. No effect on previously stated member's equity or net income.

notes.title: Subsequent Events
notes.content: Management evaluates events and transitions after balance sheet date as potential subsequent events. Management has performed this evaluation through the date of the independent auditor's report (March 24, 2025). No subsequent events to report.
